#218

MIRK, FIRK, MIRKN, and Ascher methods are using DifferentiationInterface.jl to support sparse Jacobian exploitation, shooting methods are working in progress.

Changing SparseDiffTools.jl to DifferentiationInterfce.jl doesn't cost much pain, but the performance drops comparing with the current master branch, I suspect this maybe caused by some inappropriate matrix coloring and sparsity detection usage of DifferentiationInterface.jl and SparseMatrixColorings.jl in this implementation, for example, the `jac_prototype` only need the basic structure of jacobian, but didn't find similar functionalities in DI.jl, the construction of `ColoringProblem` and `**Algorithm` in SparseMatrixColorings.jl introduce allocations, so cc Guillaume for some It looks like you are internally overwriting the `sparsity_detector` in provided `AutoSparse` backends, e.g. in `mirk.jl` L315-347. Maybe ADTypes should provide a detector/trait that can be passed to packages like BoundaryValueDiffEq.jl such that they can provide their own patterns? > Maybe ADTypes should provide a detector/trait that can be passed to packages like BoundaryValueDiffEq.jl such that they can provide their own patterns? Basically an empty ADTypes.KnownJacobianSparsityDetector.
> 
> What do you mean by that? 

Maybe users should be provided the choice between:
* `UserKnownJacobianSparsityDetector`[^1]: The user defines the pattern. This essentially is the current `KnownJacobianSparsityDetector`, a struct that holds the known pattern.
* `PackageKnownJacobianSparsityDetector`[^1]: The user wants to let the package define the pattern. This would be an empty struct to dispatch on. This is what this PR does for any `AutoSparse` backend.

> Packages are already free to subtype `AbstractSparsityDetector` if they want

Of course, every package could their own version of `PackageKnownJacobianSparsityDetector`, just like they could all define their own `AutoEnzyme` variant, but providing a common interface for (sparse) AD it is the entire purpose of ADTypes.jl.

[^1]: Disregard the awful naming of the two types, I just wanted to make clear that the current `KnownJacobianSparsityDetector` is ambiguous as to whether (a) the user or (b) the package knows the pattern.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/BoundaryValueDiffEq.jl/issues/comments/2520731855/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

(I'm guessing repository information might have previously been pulled by Pkg.)

I've tried to work around this in [BrowserMacros.jl](https://github.com/adrhill/BrowserMacros.jl/blob/main/src/method_url.jl), but haven't found out a way to obtain commit hashes to construct permalinks that aren't based on tags (which don't always exist).

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/NonlinearSolve.jl/issues/476","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/NonlinearSolve.jl","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/NonlinearSolve.jl/issues/476/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/NonlinearSolve.jl/issues/476/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/NonlinearSolve.jl/issues/476/events","html_url":"https://github.com/SciML/NonlinearSolve.jl/issues/476","id":2572251523,"node_id":"I_kwDOEV8z8M6ZUXGD","number":476,"title":"Add direct Enzyme support","user":{"login":"wsmoses","id":1260124,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEyNjAxMjQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1260124?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/wsmoses","html_url":"https://github.com/wsmoses","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wsmoses/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wsmoses/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wsmoses/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wsmoses/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wsmoses/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wsmoses/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wsmoses/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wsmoses/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wsmoses/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":5,"created_at":"2024-10-08T06:47:37Z","updated_at":"2024-10-08T15:55:14Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"AutoEnzyme should probably be specialized and not fall back to DI.

In addition to being slower in some cases, it's been shown to cause errors (even segfaults) when using AutoEnzyme in DI whereas using Enzyme directly ran successfully.

see https://github.com/jump-dev/JuMP.jl/pull/3836#discussion_r1789273432","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/NonlinearSolve.jl/issues/476/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/NonlinearSolve.jl/issues/476/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/NonlinearSolve.jl/issues/comments/2400231121","html_url":"https://github.com/SciML/NonlinearSolve.jl/issues/476#issuecomment-2400231121","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/NonlinearSolve.jl/issues/476","id":2400231121,"node_id":"IC_kwDOEV8z8M6PEJ7R","user":{"login":"adrhill","id":20258504,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwMjU4NTA0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20258504?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill","html_url":"https://github.com/adrhill","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-10-08T15:55:12Z","updated_at":"2024-10-08T15:55:12Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"I think the last bit addressing the *"maintenance of tens of copies of essentially identical Enzyme extensions"* hits the nail on the head. If maintainers had unlimited time, adding individual package extensions would be great. The appeal of DI is the centralization of that maintenance burden into a single package. We keep up with any breaking changes in any backends for package developers, freeing up dev time.

Adding specialized package extensions on top of DI is probably a valuable approach for performance critical hot loops. Individual package developers will have to decide whether the gained performance-delta is worth the increased maintenance burden. Here, Avik and Chris will have to make that call.

At the end of the day, to advance the Julia AD ecosystem, that maintenance burden should be centralized as much as possible. DI made big steps toward Enzyme with the new [argument activities](https://github.com/gdalle/DifferentiationInterface.jl/issues/311).","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/NonlinearSolve.jl/issues/comments/2400231121/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"action":"opened","number":56196,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/56196","id":2128097433,"node_id":"PR_kwDOABkWpM5-2DCZ","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56196","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56196.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56196.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56196","number":56196,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"Mark `require_one_based_indexing` and `has_offset_axes` as public","user":{"login":"adrhill","id":20258504,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwMjU4NTA0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20258504?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill","html_url":"https://github.com/adrhill","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"The discussion here mentions `require_one_based_indexing` being part of the public API: https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/43263

Both functions are also documented: 
* `require_one_based_indexing`: https://docs.julialang.org/en/v1/devdocs/offset-arrays/#man-custom-indices

quick implementation of https://github.com/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/1062#issuecomment-1952014608, (with some debug logging in it to show what happens), specifically:

> On the other hand, I wonder whether it would be best to simply call the solver with the versions of the explicitly required dependencies fixed to their declared lower bounds. In my mind, that would be simpler, safer, and more precise.
There would be no need to further increase the complexity of the resolver code, and it would actually test that the declared lower bounds work. After all, if the resolver finds that it cannot set a package version to its declared lower bound due to an incompatibility with some other required package, then the lower bound should be raised, no? Or am I missing something?


An example of its usage:

```
(PGFPlotsX) pkg> st

...

I think the first use-case is quite similar the one of `AbstractADType`: writing an interface that allows users to specify the sparsity detector

2. It's mentioned in the docstrings of the exported interface functions:
  ```markdown
    jacobian_sparsity(f, x, sd::AbstractSparsityDetector)::AbstractMatrix{Bool}
    jacobian_sparsity(f!, y, x, sd::AbstractSparsityDetector)::AbstractMatrix{Bool}
  
  Use detector sd to construct a (typically sparse) matrix S describing the pattern of nonzeroes in the Jacobian of f (resp. f!) applied
  at x (resp. (y, x)).
  ```

3. It makes it easy for users to find sparsity detectors:
  ```Julia-repl
  julia> subtypes(AbstractSparsityDetector)
  3-element Vector{Any}:
   ADTypes.KnownHessianSparsityDetector
   ADTypes.KnownJacobianSparsityDetector
   ADTypes.NoSparsityDetector
  ```
  
4. It makes stack-traces from method errors a bit shorter.


I'm not sure what is being gained by not exporting The docstring of `jacobian_sparsity` is my point 2 above. There are no references of any sparsity detectors in that docstring, only of a (currently unexpected) `AbstractSparsityDetector`. The next logical step for a user is to type `? AbstractSparsityDetector` in the REPL and get an error:

```Julia-repl
julia> using ADTypes

help?> AbstractSparsityDetector
search:

Couldn't find AbstractSparsityDetector
Perhaps you meant AbstractVector
  No documentation found.

  Binding AbstractSparsityDetector does not exist.

quick implementation of https://github.com/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/1062#issuecomment-1952014608, (with some debug logging in it to show what happens), specifically:

> On the other hand, I wonder whether it would be best to simply call the solver with the versions of the explicitly required dependencies fixed to their declared lower bounds. In my mind, that would be simpler, safer, and more precise.
There would be no need to further increase the complexity of the resolver code, and it would actually test that the declared lower bounds work. After all, if the resolver finds that it cannot set a package version to its declared lower bound due to an incompatibility with some other required package, then the lower bound should be raised, no? Or am I missing something?


An example of its usage:

```
(PGFPlotsX) pkg> st

...

I understand the argument that ideally, we should test every point of the "Pareto front" of resolvable lower bounds. But realistically speaking, this front can be very large and very expensive to test.

As far as I can tell, `julia-downgrade-compat` **only** works if the exact lower bounds specified in the compat entries are resolvable. This is equivalent to the "Pareto front" being a singular point.

From the perspective of a package developer with multiple dependencies (and possibly hundreds of indirect dependencies), this means that `julia-downgrade-compat` doesn't work out of the box. Developers must first manually set a resolvable set of lower compat entries. This manual resolving must be done by developers every time a lower bound of a compat entry is updated.

I therefore have to agree with @oxinabox on "perfect can be the enemy of good". 
Personally, I would be happy with either of these options:
1. Have `down` resolve an arbitrary lower bound environment in the same way that `up` resolves a somewhat arbitrary upper bound environment. Being able to test a single point on the "Pareto front" is already better than not testing any point at all.
2. Have `down` resolve an arbitrary lower bound environment and update the lower-bound compat entries such that the "Pareto front" is reduced to a single point and can be fully tested in CI. This is most likely overly restrictive, but it is what package developers currently have to do manually.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/issues/comments/2251056580/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"action":"closed","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/juliaup/issues/935","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/juliaup","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/juliaup/issues/935/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/juliaup/issues/935/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/juliaup/issues/935/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/juliaup/issues/935","id":2321319017,"node_id":"I_kwDOEE6KR86KXIRp","number":935,"title":"Allow users to manually modify `PATH`","user":{"login":"adrhill","id":20258504,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwMjU4NTA0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20258504?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill","html_url":"https://github.com/adrhill","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"closed","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":4,"created_at":"2024-05-28T15:06:26Z","updated_at":"2024-06-04T07:57:50Z","closed_at":"2024-06-04T07:57:49Z","author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"## Problem 

On many systems, having programs like juliaup modify the `PATH` in a `.bash_profile` or `.zshrc` config file is not a viable option.

Immutable or unreadable configuration files are the source of many crash reports, e.g. the `"Failed to open file"` error (#826, #864, #874, #891, #918, ...).

In [#796](https://github.com/JuliaLang/juliaup/issues/796#issuecomment-1909447404), @davidanthoff  mentioned

> So in both cases it looks like we don't have the permissions required to edit the shell scripts where add Juliaup to the PATH. We can (and should) obviously catch this and output something more helpful, but at the same time I'm not sure how we can recover from this error, putting things onto the PATH is really crucial for things to work, but if we can't do that, then that is kind of a problem...
>
> If anyone has an idea, that would be great :)


## Feature request

juliaup should provide an option for advanced users where it simply tells them what needs to be manually added to their `PATH`.

Such an option seemingly exists, but doesn't appear to work (#875), still running into the `"Failed to open file"` error:
```
✔ Do you want to add the Julia binaries to your PATH by manipulating various shell startup scripts? · no
```

This has also been observed in #920:

> Strangely, the crash during the juliaup installation also occurs if I customize the installation to decline "Do you want to add the Julia binaries to your PATH by manipulating various shell startup scripts?". 

Related discussions: #796, #920, #875

seems a lot of the niceness that has been added for "stackoverflow type" of errors (or just generally when a function is called recursively) has been lost: As an example: ```julia julia> f(x) = g(x) f (generic function with 1 method) julia> g(x) = f(x) g (generic function with 1 method) ``` 1.7: ```julia julia> f(10) ERROR: StackOverflowError: Stacktrace: [1] f(x::Int64) @ Main ./REPL[1]:1 [2] g(x::Int64) @ Main ./REPL[2]:1 --- the last 2 lines are repeated 39990 more times --- [79983] f(x::Int64) @ Main ./REPL[1]:1 ``` 1.8+: ```julia julia> f(10) ERROR: StackOverflowError: Stacktrace: [1] g(x::Int64) @ Main ./REPL[2]:1 [2] f(x::Int64) @ Main ./REPL[1]:1 ``` The ``` --- the last 2 lines are repeated 39990 more times --- [79983] f(x::Int64) ``` part is lost. -------------- Another example: ```julia julia> f(x) = f(x) f (generic function with 1 method) ``` 1.7: ```julia julia> f(1) ERROR: StackOverflowError: Stacktrace: [1] f(x::Int64) (repeats 79984 times) @ Main ./REPL[1]:1 ``` 1.8: ```julia julia> f(1) ERROR: StackOverflowError: Stacktrace: [1] f(x::Int64) (repeats 2 times) @ Main ./REPL[4]:1 ``` 1.8 says `repeats 2 times` which seems wrong.. working"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-05-16T07:38:31Z","updated_at":"2024-05-16T07:45:11Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"COLLABORATOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"The recent PR #54 by @adrhill, which was merged by @ChrisRackauckas before I had time to review it, makes things disappear from the public API in a breaking way. In Julia, the documentation _is_ the public API, so we cannot edit docstrings quite however we want.

The things that need to be done are:

- Explicitly document "fields" instead of "keyword arguments", because otherwise users of ADTypes.jl (like DifferentiationInterface.jl) have no API-sanctioned way to access the specifics of a backend object
- Same for type parameters like `AutoChainRules{RC}`, which have disappeared in the PR.

Essentially, I'd prefer to revert the entire PR and then add back this precision everywhere:

```
Struct used to select the ... backend for automatic differentiation.
Exported from ADTypes.jl

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/juliaup/issues/935","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/juliaup","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/juliaup/issues/935/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/juliaup/issues/935/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/juliaup/issues/935/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/juliaup/issues/935","id":2321319017,"node_id":"I_kwDOEE6KR86KXIRp","number":935,"title":"Allow users to manually modify `PATH`","user":{"login":"adrhill","id":20258504,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwMjU4NTA0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20258504?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill","html_url":"https://github.com/adrhill","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2024-05-28T15:06:26Z","updated_at":"2024-05-30T11:37:45Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"## Problem 

On many systems, having programs like juliaup modify the `PATH` in a `.bash_profile` or `.zshrc` config file is not a viable option.

Immutable or unreadable configuration files are the source of many crash reports, e.g. the `"Failed to open file"` error (#826, #864, #874, #891, #918, ...).

In [#796](https://github.com/JuliaLang/juliaup/issues/796#issuecomment-1909447404), @davidanthoff  mentioned

> So in both cases it looks like we don't have the permissions required to edit the shell scripts where add Juliaup to the PATH. We can (and should) obviously catch this and output something more helpful, but at the same time I'm not sure how we can recover from this error, putting things onto the PATH is really crucial for things to work, but if we can't do that, then that is kind of a problem...
>
> If anyone has an idea, that would be great :)


## Feature request

juliaup should provide an option for advanced users where it simply tells them what needs to be manually added to their `PATH`.

Such an option seemingly exists, but doesn't appear to work (#875), still running into the `"Failed to open file"` error:
```
✔ Do you want to add the Julia binaries to your PATH by manipulating various shell startup scripts? · no
```

This has also been observed in #920:

> Strangely, the crash during the juliaup installation also occurs if I customize the installation to decline "Do you want to add the Julia binaries to your PATH by manipulating various shell startup scripts?". 

Related discussions: #796, #920, #875
","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/juliaup/issues/935/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/juliaup/issues/935/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/juliaup/issues/comments/2139365081","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/juliaup/issues/935#issuecomment-2139365081","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/juliaup/issues/935","id":2139365081,"node_id":"IC_kwDOEE6KR85_hB7Z","user":{"login":"adrhill","id":20258504,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwMjU4NTA0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20258504?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill","html_url":"https://github.com/adrhill","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-05-30T11:37:44Z","updated_at":"2024-05-30T11:37:44Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"Even with #844 addressed, I still find it surprising that the "manual installation option" tries to open any shell config files. {"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ADTypes.jl/issues/38","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ADTypes.jl","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ADTypes.jl/issues/38/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ADTypes.jl/issues/38/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ADTypes.jl/issues/38/events","html_url":"https://github.com/SciML/ADTypes.jl/issues/38","id":2235947549,"node_id":"I_kwDOJBx6J86FRdod","number":38,"title":"Change handling of sparse backends","user":{"login":"gdalle","id":22795598,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIyNzk1NTk4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/22795598?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/gdalle","html_url":"https://github.com/gdalle","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gdalle/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gdalle/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gdalle/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gdalle/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gdalle/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gdalle/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gdalle/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gdalle/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gdalle/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":14,"created_at":"2024-04-10T16:01:15Z","updated_at":"2024-04-10T18:34:01Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"COLLABORATOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Problems:

- The code for `AutoSparseBackend` is a duplicate of `AutoBackend`, sometimes with misses (see #33)
- There is no built-in way to specify additional aspects like the sparsity detection method or the coloring algorithm
- It is tedious to enumerate all possible sparse backends

Suggested solutions (instead of #37):

- Replace `AutoSparseBackend` with a wrapper struct `AutoSparse(AutoBackend)`
- Add fields to this wrapper struct for sparse-specific ingredients

```julia
struct AutoSparse{B<:AbstractADType,S,C} <: AbstractADType
    backend::B
    sparsity_detector::S
    coloring_algorithm::C
end
```

To preserve backwards-compatibility we can try to define shortcuts like the following, to be removed in v0.3.0:

```julia
const AutoSparseBackend = AutoSparse{<:AutoBackend}

function AutoSparseBackend(args...; sparsity_detector, coloring_algorithm, kwargs...)
    backend = AutoBackend(args...; kwargs...)
    return AutoSparse(backend, sparsity_detector, coloring_algorithm)
end
```

However I'm not 100% sure we can keep this from being breaking.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ADTypes.jl/issues/38/reactions","total_count":1,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":1,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ADTypes.jl/issues/38/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ADTypes.jl/issues/comments/2048205153","html_url":"https://github.com/SciML/ADTypes.jl/issues/38#issuecomment-2048205153","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/ADTypes.jl/issues/38","id":2048205153,"node_id":"IC_kwDOJBx6J856FSFh","user":{"login":"adrhill","id":20258504,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwMjU4NTA0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20258504?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill","html_url":"https://github.com/adrhill","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-04-10T18:34:00Z","updated_at":"2024-04-10T18:34:00Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"> This is based on input propagation and won't work (give incorrect pattern) for control flow or branching that affects sparsity patterns, right?

We could in theory provide a second dual number tracer that uses primal values for control flow / branches. 

Currently, SparseConnectivityTracer returns input-output connectivities, which are a conservative estimate of the Jacobian sparsity. We could be a bit smarter in our operator overloading for functions like `round` that return zero-derivatives.

behavior"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2022-11-25T19:51:48Z","updated_at":"2024-02-28T16:30:49Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"`Base.url` currently only returns a valid URL when applied to Julia base methods. 
For functions from stdlib and external packages, a path to the local file is returned instead of a URL.

The relevant branch of `Base.url` looks like dead code to me:
https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/04214ece63e472fada63f2ff961b5d10d925669a/base/methodshow.jl#L374-L396

(I'm guessing repository information might have previously been pulled by Pkg.)

I've tried to work around this in [BrowserMacros.jl](https://github.com/adrhill/BrowserMacros.jl/blob/main/src/method_url.jl), but haven't found out a way to obtain commit hashes to construct permalinks that aren't based on tags (which don't always exist).

#47708 introduced a bug in the edge case of zero-length Markdown headers. 
Since the exponent of `char^(line_width-margin)` becomes negative, Julia errors:
```Julia
julia> md"# "
  Error showing value of type Markdown.MD:
ERROR: ArgumentError: can't repeat a character -1 times
```

Comparing this PR to the last release (#47632):
| Release | PR    |
|:-------:|:-----:| 
| ![release](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/20258504/204249379-cce7342c-6cc2-4b8b-9552-c79940a97a79.png) | ![pr](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/20258504/204249389-ac4dab15-5bc6-4c8c-b360-6eda4055a5df.png) |

If the single `≡` is desired, we can set the minimum `header_width` to one.
I can also add back the extra newline created by zero-length-underlines.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/47725/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/47725/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1357871433","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/47725#issuecomment-1357871433","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/47725","id":1357871433,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM5Q73lJ","user":{"login":"adrhill","id":20258504,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwMjU4NTA0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20258504?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill","html_url":"https://github.com/adrhill","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adrhill/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2022-12-19T15:49:48Z","updated_at":"2022-12-19T15:49:48Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"Bump","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1357871433/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

This is noticeable in the "Examples" header of doc strings, e.g.:
```Julia-repl
help?> +
search: +

  +(x, y...)


  Addition operator. x+y+z+... calls this function with all arguments, i.e. +(x, y, z, ...).

  Examples
  ≡≡≡≡≡≡≡≡≡≡
```

In case this isn't a stylistic choice,
https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/d12ac369a4be6ae7833fb95c644cea4531764e31/stdlib/Markdown/src/render/terminal/render.jl#L91

should be replaced by `char^(line_width-margin)`.

I would gladly open a PR in case this is a bug.

For functions from stdlib and external packages, a path to the local file is returned instead of a URL.

The relevant branch of `Base.url` looks like dead code to me:
https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/04214ece63e472fada63f2ff961b5d10d925669a/base/methodshow.jl#L374-L396

(I'm guessing repository information might have previously been pulled by Pkg.)

I've tried to work around this in [BrowserMacros.jl](https://github.com/adrhill/BrowserMacros.jl/blob/main/src/method_url.jl), but haven't found out a way to obtain commit hashes to construct permalinks that aren't based on tags (which don't always exist).
